channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Harnessing highly efficient coherent polariton parametric emission in quantum confined perovskite microcavities
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 02 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71322-1

Microcavity exciton polaritons are a promising platform for optical parametric processes. Here, authors achieve efficient parametric emission with strong phase coherence in quantum confined microcavities, demonstrating a polariton supersolid phase at room temperature.
item.dc:title: Harnessing highly efficient coherent polariton parametric emission in quantum confined perovskite microcavities
item.dc:creator: Xinyi Deng
Sanjib Ghosh
Jiepeng Song
Changhai Zhu
Chengyong Yu
Qinglin Jia
Kangshu Li
Chun Li
Xiaoxu Zhao
Xinfeng Liu
Qing Zhang

item.title: Spontaneous emergence of Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interaction via field-cooling-induced interface engineering in 2D ferromagnetic ternary tellurides
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 02 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71294-2

Fe3GaTe2 is a room temperature van der Waals ferromagnet, which, despite having a centrosymmetric crystal structure, has both a large Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interaction and hosts topological spin textures. Here, Xia, Luo, Malik and coauthors unravel this mystery, showing the critical role of field cooling in the breaking of inversion symmetry in Fe3GaTe2.
item.dc:title: Spontaneous emergence of Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interaction via field-cooling-induced interface engineering in 2D ferromagnetic ternary tellurides
item.dc:creator: Shian Xia
Yan Luo
Iftikhar Ahmed Malik
Xinyi Zhou
Keying Han
Yue Sun
Haoyun Lin
Hanqing Shi
Xiaoze Liu
Yingchun Cheng
Vanessa Li Zhang
Yi Du
Sheng Liu
Chao Zhu
Ting Yu
